-- Create the custom function
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION what_quarter_is(date_in DATE, start_month INTEGER DEFAULT 1)
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN FLOOR(
        (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date_in) - start_month + 12) % 12 / 3.0
    )::INTEGER + 1;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE;

-- Example usage with your date
SELECT what_quarter_is('2001-02-16'::DATE, 4) AS quarter;

-- Or for the trans table with April as Q1 start:
SELECT 
    trans_id,
    date,
    what_quarter_is(date, 4) AS fiscal_quarter,
    amount
FROM trans
ORDER BY date
LIMIT 10

WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    -- Convert purchase_date to proper date format and get distinct user-month combinations
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD'))::DATE AS month_date
    FROM purchase
),
streak_groups AS (
    -- Identify consecutive month groups using the difference between row number and month sequence
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        month_date,
        month_date - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date) || ' months')::INTERVAL AS streak_group
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    -- Calculate the length of each streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        streak_group,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY user_id, streak_group
),
longest_streaks AS (
    -- Get the longest streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
-- Count how many users have each longest streak length
SELECT 
    longest_streak AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM longest_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY streak_length

WITH ranked_banks AS (
    SELECT 
        trans_id,
        account_id,
        date,
        bank,
        CASE 
            WHEN bank IS NOT NULL THEN 
                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY date, trans_id)
        END AS bank_group
    FROM trans
),
filled_banks AS (
    SELECT 
        trans_id,
        account_id,
        date,
        bank,
        MAX(bank_group) OVER (
            PARTITION BY account_id 
            ORDER BY date, trans_id 
            ROWS BETWEEN CURRENT ROW AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
        ) AS next_bank_group
    FROM ranked_banks
)
SELECT 
    fb.trans_id,
    fb.account_id,
    fb.date,
    fb.bank,
    rb.bank AS next_bank
FROM filled_banks fb
LEFT JOIN ranked_banks rb 
    ON fb.account_id = rb.account_id 
    AND fb.next_bank_group = rb.bank_group

SELECT 
    JSON_OBJECT_AGG(
        label, 
        molecules
    ) AS result
FROM (
    SELECT 
        m.label,
        JSON_AGG(
            JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
                'molecule_id', m.molecule_id,
                'atoms', COALESCE(atoms_data, '[]'::json),
                'bonds', COALESCE(bonds_data, '[]'::json)
            )
        ) AS molecules
    FROM molecule m
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT 
            molecule_id,
            JSON_AGG(
                JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
                    'atom_id', atom_id,
                    'element', element
                )
            ) AS atoms_data
        FROM atom
        GROUP BY molecule_id
    ) a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT 
            molecule_id,
            JSON_AGG(
                JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
                    'bond_id', bond_id,
                    'bond_type', bond_type
                )
            ) AS bonds_data
        FROM bond
        GROUP BY molecule_id
    ) b ON m.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
    GROUP BY m.label
) grouped_data

SELECT 
    player_id,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'goals' THEN stat_value END) AS goals,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'assists' THEN stat_value END) AS assists,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'yellow_cards' THEN stat_value END) AS yellow_cards,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'red_cards' THEN stat_value END) AS red_cards,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'shots' THEN stat_value END) AS shots,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'passes' THEN stat_value END) AS passes
FROM (
    SELECT 
        player_id,
        TRIM(split_part(stats_keys, ',', n)) AS stat_key,
        TRIM(split_part(stats_values, ',', n)) AS stat_value
    FROM player_stats
    CROSS JOIN (
        SELECT 1 AS n UNION ALL
        SELECT 2 UNION ALL
        SELECT 3 UNION ALL
        SELECT 4 UNION ALL
        SELECT 5 UNION ALL
        SELECT 6
    ) AS numbers
    WHERE split_part(stats_keys, ',', n) != ''
) AS unpivoted
GROUP BY player_id
ORDER BY player_id

SELECT 
    base_table.relname as base_table,
    base_attr.attname as base_col,
    ref_table.relname as referencing_table,
    ref_attr.attname as referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) as constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN pg_class base_table ON c.confrelid = base_table.oid
JOIN pg_class ref_table ON c.conrelid = ref_table.oid
JOIN LATERAL unnest(c.confkey, c.conkey) WITH ORDINALITY AS keys(base_attnum, ref_attnum, ord) ON true
JOIN pg_attribute base_attr ON base_attr.attrelid = c.confrelid AND base_attr.attnum = keys.base_attnum
JOIN pg_attribute ref_attr ON ref_attr.attrelid = c.conrelid AND ref_attr.attnum = keys.ref_attnum

WITH team_matches AS (

            ELSE 'D'
        END as result
    FROM team t

    SELECT 
        t.team_api_id,
        t.team_long_name,

            WHEN m.away_team_goal > m.home_team_goal THEN 'W'
            WHEN m.away_team_goal < m.home_team_goal THEN 'L'

    FROM team t
    JOIN match m ON t.team_api_id = m.away_team_api_id
)
SELECT 
    team_long_name,
    COUNT(*) as matches,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE result = 'W') as victories,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE result = 'L') as defeats,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE result = 'D') as draws,

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Base case: Start from cards that have no previous card (head of chains)
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    c.id::TEXT AS Path,
    c.id AS start_id
  FROM collection c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM collection c2 
    WHERE c2.nextCardId = c.id
  )
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: Follow the nextCardId chain forward
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    cte.Path || ' --> ' || c.id::TEXT,
    cte.start_id
  FROM collection c
  JOIN path_cte cte ON cte.nextCardId = c.id
)
SELECT Path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextCardId IS NULL  -- Only show complete paths (ending at tail nodes)
ORDER BY start_id

WITH parent_posts AS (
  SELECT 
    p1.id as parent_id,
    p1.acceptedanswerid as child_id,
    p1.score as parent_score,
    p2.score as child_score
  FROM posts p1
  LEFT JOIN posts p2 ON p1.acceptedanswerid = p2.id
  WHERE p1.acceptedanswerid IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.id, p.acceptedanswerid, p.posttypeid, p.score
FROM posts p
WHERE 
  -- Include parent posts with score >= 20
  (p.id IN (SELECT parent_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_score >= 20))
  OR
  -- Include child posts where parent score < 20 but child score >= 20
  (p.id IN (SELECT child_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_score < 20 AND child_score >= 20))
  OR
  -- Include posts with score >= 20 that are not involved in any parent-child relationship
  (p.score >= 20 
   AND p.id NOT IN (SELECT parent_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_id IS NOT NULL)
   AND p.id NOT IN (SELECT child_id FROM parent_posts WHERE child_id IS NOT NULL))
ORDER BY p.id

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Get path from Superhero A to root
path_a AS (
    SELECT id, parent_id, 0 as depth
    FROM lineage 
    WHERE id = 1001
    UNION ALL
    SELECT l.id, l.parent_id, pa.depth + 1
    FROM lineage l
    INNER JOIN path_a pa ON pa.parent_id = l.id
    WHERE l.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
-- Get path from Superhero B to root
path_b AS (
    SELECT id, parent_id, 0 as depth
    FROM lineage 
    WHERE id = 1201
    UNION ALL
    SELECT l.id, l.parent_id, pb.depth + 1
    FROM lineage l
    INNER JOIN path_b pb ON pb.parent_id = l.id
    WHERE l.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
-- Find common ancestors
common_ancestors AS (
    SELECT pa.id, pa.depth as depth_a, pb.depth as depth_b
    FROM path_a pa
    INNER JOIN path_b pb ON pa.id = pb.id
)
-- Get the lowest common ancestor (closest to the superheroes)
SELECT id as common_ancestor_id
FROM common_ancestors
ORDER BY depth_a + depth_b
LIMIT 1

WITH RECURSIVE EmpMgrCTE AS (
    -- Anchor: Start with each employee and their direct information
    SELECT 
        id as employeeId,
        salary as employeeSalary,
        bossid,
        0 as hierarchical_level,
        bossid as currentBossId,
        CAST(NULL AS INTEGER) as bossSalary
    FROM staff
    WHERE bossid IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: Traverse up the hierarchy
    SELECT 
        e.employeeId,
        e.employeeSalary,
        s.bossid,
        e.hierarchical_level + 1,
        s.id as currentBossId,
        s.salary as bossSalary
    FROM EmpMgrCTE e
    INNER JOIN staff s ON e.bossid = s.id
    WHERE s.salary < 2 * e.employeeSalary  -- Continue only if condition not met
)
SELECT 
    employeeId,
    employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    currentBossId as bossId,
    bossSalary
FROM EmpMgrCTE
WHERE bossSalary >= 2 * employeeSalary  -- Filter for the salary condition
ORDER BY employeeId, hierarchical_level

WITH qr1(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup1 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
), 
qr2(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup2 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
combined AS (
    SELECT qr1.item, qr1.subgroup as subgroup1, qr2.subgroup as subgroup2 
    FROM qr1 
    FULL OUTER JOIN qr2 ON qr1.item = qr2.item
),
-- Create edges between items that share subgroups
edges AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT c1.item as item1, c2.item as item2
    FROM combined c1
    JOIN combined c2 ON (c1.subgroup1 = c2.subgroup1 OR c1.subgroup2 = c2.subgroup2)
    WHERE c1.item < c2.item
),
-- Find connected components using recursive CTE
components AS (
    SELECT item, item as group_id
    FROM combined
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT c.item, LEAST(c.group_id, e.item2) as group_id
    FROM components c
    JOIN edges e ON c.item = e.item1
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT c.item, LEAST(c.group_id, e.item1) as group_id
    FROM components c
    JOIN edges e ON c.item = e.item2
)
SELECT 
    c.item, 
    c.subgroup1, 
    c.subgroup2,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY MIN(comp.group_id)) as group_id
FROM combined c
JOIN components comp ON c.item = comp.item
GROUP BY c.item, c.subgroup1, c.subgroup2
ORDER BY group_id, c.item

WITH LastTransactionPerStation AS (
  -- Get the last transaction at each gas station for each customer
  SELECT
    *,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid 
      ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC, transactionid DESC
    ) AS rn_per_station
  FROM transactions_1k
),
FilteredTransactions AS (
  -- Keep only the last transaction per gas station
  SELECT *
  FROM LastTransactionPerStation
  WHERE rn_per_station = 1
),
CustomerIssues AS (
  -- Identify if customer has any amount < 10 issues and find the first one chronologically
  SELECT
    customerid,
    MIN(CASE 
      WHEN amount < 10 THEN date || ' ' || time || ' ' || LPAD(transactionid::text, 20, '0')
    END) AS first_issue_key
  FROM FilteredTransactions
  GROUP BY customerid
),
FinalSelection AS (
  SELECT
    ft.*,
    ci.first_issue_key,
    date || ' ' || time || ' ' || LPAD(transactionid::text, 20, '0') AS current_key,
    -- For customers without issues, find their last transaction overall
    MAX(date || ' ' || time || ' ' || LPAD(transactionid::text, 20, '0')) 
      OVER (PARTITION BY ft.customerid) AS last_transaction_key
  FROM FilteredTransactions ft
  LEFT JOIN CustomerIssues ci ON ft.customerid = ci.customerid
)
SELECT
  customerid,
  transactionid,
  gasstationid,
  amount,
  date,
  time,
  price
FROM FinalSelection
WHERE
  -- If customer has an issue (amount < 10), show the first occurrence
  (first_issue_key IS NOT NULL AND current_key = first_issue_key)
  OR
  -- If customer has no issues, show the last transaction
  (first_issue_key IS NULL AND current_key = last_transaction_key)
ORDER BY customerid, date, time

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    UPDATE team_attributes ta
    SET eff_to = subquery.next_date
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            id,
            COALESCE(
                LEAD(TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE, 1) OVER (
                    PARTITION BY team_api_id 
                    ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE ASC
                ),
                TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ) - INTERVAL '1 day' AS next_date
        FROM team_attributes
        WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE ta.id = subquery.id
    AND ta.team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id;
    
    RETURN NEW;
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes 
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

WITH RECURSIVE bifurcate AS (
    -- Base case: first chunk for each attribute
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 50
            ELSE attribute_value
        END AS attribute_chunk,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN attribute_value - 50
            ELSE 0
        END AS remaining,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS is_bifurcated
    FROM hero_attribute
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: continue splitting remaining value
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN remaining > 50 THEN 50
            ELSE remaining
        END AS attribute_chunk,
        CASE 
            WHEN remaining > 50 THEN remaining - 50
            ELSE 0
        END AS remaining,
        1 AS is_bifurcated
    FROM bifurcate
    WHERE remaining > 0
)

        releasedate,
        releasedate - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY releasedate) * INTERVAL '1 day') AS grp
    FROM distinct_dates
),
consecutive_periods AS (
    -- Group by the calculated difference to find consecutive periods
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS count,
        MIN(releasedate) AS date_min,
        MAX(releasedate) AS date_max
    FROM grouped_dates
    GROUP BY grp
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1  -- Only periods with more than 1 day
)

FROM consecutive_periods

-- Create the state transition function for forward fill
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION GapFillInternal(s anyelement, v anyelement) 
RETURNS anyelement AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    IF v IS NOT NULL THEN 
        RETURN v;
    ELSIF s IS NOT NULL THEN 
        RETURN s;
    ELSE 
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE PLPGSQL;

-- Create the aggregate function
CREATE AGGREGATE GapFill(anyelement) (
    SFUNC = GapFillInternal, 
    STYPE = anyelement
);

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id, 
        amount, 
        amount as cum_sum, 
        1 as bucket 
    FROM transfer 
    WHERE id = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        transfer.id, 
        transfer.amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN cte.cum_sum + transfer.amount > 20 THEN transfer.amount 
            ELSE cte.cum_sum + transfer.amount 
        END as cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN cte.cum_sum + transfer.amount > 20 THEN cte.bucket + 1 
            ELSE cte.bucket 
        END as bucket
    FROM cte 
    JOIN transfer ON transfer.id = cte.id + 1
)
SELECT 
    id, 
    amount,
    cum_sum, 
    bucket 
FROM cte 
ORDER BY id

WITH RECURSIVE cumulative_result AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        ROUND((trx * (1 + event))::numeric, 6) as result,
        1 as rn
    FROM sales_transactions
    ORDER BY id
    LIMIT 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case
    SELECT 
        st.id,
        st.trx,
        st.event,
        ROUND(((cr.result + st.trx) * (1 + st.event))::numeric, 6) as result,
        cr.rn + 1
    FROM cumulative_result cr
    INNER JOIN sales_transactions st ON st.id > cr.id
    WHERE st.id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM sales_transactions WHERE id > cr.id)
)
SELECT 
    id,
    result
FROM cumulative_result
ORDER BY id

WITH pivoted_changes AS (

    FROM player_stats_changes

),
filled_changes AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        updated_at,
        COALESCE(
            height,
            LAST_VALUE(height IGNORE NULLS) OVER (
                PARTITION BY entity_id 
                ORDER BY updated_at 
                ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
            )
        ) as height,
        COALESCE(
            weight,
            LAST_VALUE(weight IGNORE NULLS) OVER (
                PARTITION BY entity_id 
                ORDER BY updated_at 
                ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
            )
        ) as weight
    FROM pivoted_changes
)
SELECT entity_id, height, weight, updated_at
FROM filled_changes
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: Start with leaf nodes (nodes that have color already set)
    SELECT id, parent_id, color
    FROM superhero2
    WHERE color IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Process parent nodes based on their children's colors
    SELECT 
        s.id, 
        s.parent_id,
        CASE 
            -- If parent has only one child, take that child's color
            WHEN COUNT(c.id) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)
            -- If parent has two children with same color, take that color
            WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)
            -- If parent has two children with different colors, make it gray
            ELSE 'gray'
        END AS color
    FROM superhero2 s
    INNER JOIN cte c ON s.id = c.parent_id
    WHERE s.color IS NULL
    GROUP BY s.id, s.parent_id
)
UPDATE superhero2
SET color = cte.color
FROM cte
WHERE superhero2.id = cte.id AND superhero2.color IS NULL

    JOIN nodes ON nodes.uuid = c.parent_uuid
)

WITH RECURSIVE item_costs AS (
    -- Base case: Direct resource costs and immediate item costs
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price,
        uses_item_id,
        uses_item_price,
        resource_id,
        resource_price,
        quantity,
        0 as depth
    FROM store
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Expand items used in crafting
    SELECT 
        ic.item_id,
        ic.item_price,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.uses_item_price,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        ic.quantity * s.quantity as quantity,
        ic.depth + 1
    FROM item_costs ic
    JOIN store s ON ic.uses_item_id = s.item_id
    WHERE ic.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
      AND ic.depth < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
)
SELECT 
    s.item_id,
    s.item_price as retail_price,
    -- Partial craft price: only direct dependencies (resources + items at face value)
    SUM(DISTINCT COALESCE(s.uses_item_price * s.quantity, 0)) + 
    SUM(DISTINCT COALESCE(s.resource_price * s.quantity, 0)) as partial_craft_price,
    -- Full craft price: recursively calculated costs
    (SELECT SUM(COALESCE(resource_price * quantity, 0))
     FROM item_costs 
     WHERE item_costs.item_id = s.item_id 
       AND resource_id IS NOT NULL) as full_craft_price
FROM store s
GROUP BY s.item_id, s.item_price
ORDER BY s.item_id

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        to_date(fe.start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
        to_date(fe.end_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code
    FROM fact_collection fe
    INNER JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
month_series AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
        (SELECT MIN(to_date(start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD')) FROM fact_collection),
        CURRENT_DATE,
        INTERVAL '1 month'
    ) AS month_date
)
SELECT 
    date_trunc('month', ms.month_date) AS month,
    ca.set_name,
    COUNT(*) AS existing_cards
FROM month_series ms
CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT set_name, set_code FROM card_activity) AS sets
LEFT JOIN card_activity ca 
    ON ca.set_name = sets.set_name
    AND ca.start_date <= ms.month_date
    AND (ca.end_date IS NULL OR ca.end_date >= date_trunc('month', ms.month_date))
GROUP BY date_trunc('month', ms.month_date), ca.set_name
ORDER BY month, ca.set_name

WITH user_groups AS (
  -- First, find all connected users through shared refs
  SELECT DISTINCT
    u1.id as user_id,
    COALESCE(u2.ref1, u1.ref1, u2.ref2, u1.ref2) as group_ref
  FROM users u1
  LEFT JOIN users u2 ON u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref1 = u2.ref2 
                     OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2
),
merged_users AS (
  -- Group users by their shared reference
  SELECT 
    MIN(group_ref) as group_id,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT user_id) as ids
  FROM user_groups
  GROUP BY group_ref
),
asset_ownership AS (
  -- Get assets with their owners, including orphaned assets
  SELECT 
    a.id as asset_id,
    a.owner,
    u.id as user_id
  FROM assets a
  LEFT JOIN users u ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
)
SELECT 
  COALESCE(mu.ids, ARRAY[]::integer[]) as ids,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.username) FILTER (WHERE u.username IS NOT NULL) as usernames,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1) FILTER (WHERE u.ref1 IS NOT NULL) as refs1,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT COALESCE(u.ref2, ao.owner)) as refs2,
  COUNT(DISTINCT ao.asset_id) as asset_count,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ao.asset_id ORDER BY ao.asset_id) as asset_ids
FROM asset_ownership ao
LEFT JOIN merged_users mu ON ao.user_id = ANY(mu.ids)
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = ANY(COALESCE(mu.ids, ARRAY[ao.user_id]))
GROUP BY mu.group_id, ao.owner, mu.ids
ORDER BY MIN(ao.asset_id)
